title: Life and Translation of Saint Thomas of Canterbury
summary: The repository XML is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME00142, which encodes Hermann Thiemke's 1919 critical edition of the South English Legendary Life of Thomas Becket and the discrete seventy-four-line Translation of Thomas Becket printed as lines 2455-2528. Thiemke based the b opening and shared continuation primarily on Bodleian MS Ashmole 43, supplied the mutually exclusive a opening from the Laud-family text printed by Horstmann, directly collated ten further witnesses, and used Black's 1845 Harley 2277 transcript as a second printed intermediary. No single witness supplies both alternative openings. The manifest separates this thirteen-witness editorial corpus from DIMEV's broader modern census: record 1507 lists sixteen Life witnesses, and record 4768 has fifteen active Translation occurrences in fourteen physical manuscripts because Rawlinson poet. 225 contains two copies. Complete public copies of all three editions are cached. Complete provider-exposed manuscript facsimiles are cached for Ashmole 43, Laud Misc. 108, Bodley 779, the Vernon Manuscript, CCCC MS 145, and Trinity R.3.25; exact work loci inside each complete codex are mapped. Laud Misc. 463 and Add. C. 220 have only non-overlapping selected images, while Lambeth MS 223 has selected images that reproduce the Translation boundary but not the whole Life. Exact catalogs, dated negative public-access findings, and reproduction routes are recorded for the remaining editorial and collateral witnesses.
review_notes: The U-M encoding is composite: its two alternate birth openings and shared Life continuation are followed without a new HEAD by the separately indexed Translation of Thomas Becket at Thiemke lines 2455-2528.
Thiemke's direct and mediated consultation routes are not conflated. He says he copied M, E, C, S, V, L, D, R, G, J, and B; H and W reached him through Horstmann 1887 and Black 1845 respectively.

The critical edition serializes both mutually exclusive birth redactions before their shared continuation. A reading path chooses either a or b; the XML's physical line count therefore exceeds the line count of either coherent version.
Manuscript dates, places, and dialects describe individual witnesses and are not silently inherited as composition facts about the anonymous work.

A complete local IIIF bundle contains every canvas published by the provider. Its work_portion maps the relevant leaves but does not imply that every medieval leaf survives or that the provider's digital sequence is physically exhaustive beyond its own completeness claim.
Local caching records research access and fixity, not permission to redistribute provider images. Rights statements remain scoped to named textual, image, catalog, or digital components.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: Thiemke's base witness M. The Life occupies ff. 228v-259r and the Translation ff. 259r-260r.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: Thiemke's H witness, available to him through Horstmann's edition. The Life occupies ff. 61r-87v and the Translation ff. 87v-88r.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.value: 1290-1400, with the relevant core late thirteenth or early fourteenth century

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2277
entities.description: Thiemke's W witness, available to him through Black's edition. The Life occupies ff. 195v-226 and the Translation ff. 226-227.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1993
entities.description: Thiemke's E witness. The Life occupies ff. 64r-91v; no Translation copy is identified.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 145
entities.description: Thiemke's C witness. The Life occupies ff. 180r-210v and the Translation ff. 217v-218.

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 949
entities.description: Thiemke's S witness. The Life occupies ff. 110v-141r; DIMEV places the Translation at ff. 141v-144, while the current catalog's next-item boundary requires verification.

date_statements.label: older Stowe catalog date reported by Thiemke

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: Thiemke's V witness. The Life survives imperfectly at ff. 73rb-80vb before lost leaves 81-88; no Translation survives in this copy.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: Thiemke's L witness. A manuscript-specific catalog places its Gilbert-omitting Life from f. 141r to the Translation boundary at f. 156va and the Translation at ff. 156va-157rb.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Cleopatra D IX
entities.description: Thiemke's D witness. The Life occupies ff. 118r-148v and the Translation ff. 148v-149v.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25
entities.description: Thiemke's R witness. The Life occupies ff. 188v-214 and the Translation ff. 214-215; the exact side of the internal boundary remains unresolved.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 223
entities.description: Thiemke's G witness. The Life occupies ff. 257v-290v; the current catalog begins the Translation in column 290b and the following Silvester item in column 291b.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Julius D IX
entities.description: Thiemke's J witness. The Life occupies ff. 232v-265v and the Translation ff. 265v-266v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 779
entities.description: Thiemke's B witness. The Life occupies ff. 41v-66v; a surviving Translation copy is not established for this manuscript despite a misleading DIMEV linkage.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Add. C. 38
entities.description: Collateral Harley-redaction witness not used by Thiemke. The Life occupies ff. 5v-33v and the Translation ff. 33-34; the two works share f. 33 and the Translation rubric is reported on f. 33v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Add. C. 220
entities.description: Collateral Harley-record witness not used by Thiemke. Its Gilbert-opening-omitting Life occupies ff. 8v-18v and ends imperfectly; no Translation copy is identified.

date_statements.label: Taylor's description
date_statements.value: late fourteenth or early fifteenth century

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. c. 3
entities.description: Collateral fragmentary Harley-redaction witness not used by Thiemke. Only the first sixty lines of the Life survive at f. 3r-v within the manuscript's third binding-fragment part.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Add. 3039
entities.description: Collateral Harley-record witness not used by Thiemke. The Gilbert-opening-omitting Life occupies ff. 80v-104v and the Translation ff. 104v-105v.

entities.label: Magdalene College, Pepys Library MS 2344
entities.description: Collateral witness not used by Thiemke. The Life occupies pp. 41-90, with terminal damage reported, while a discontiguous Translation fragment of only twenty-six lines survives at p. 528.

date_statements.label: Manuscripts of the West Midlands date
date_statements.value: second half of the fourteenth century

entities.label: British Library Add MS 24078, Becket binder-waste fragments
entities.description: Collateral Harley-record fragments not used by Thiemke. Four leaves from a lost fourteenth-century English legendary were reused as binder waste in a later law volume; Becket fragments are now mounted at ff. 275rb-276va.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 2810
entities.description: Collateral witness not used by Thiemke. The Life occupies ff. 145v-163v and the Translation ff. 163v-164, sharing f. 163v.

entities.label: College of Arms Arundel MS VIII
entities.description: Collateral Harley-redaction witness not used by Thiemke. The Life occupies ff. 80v-116v and ends imperfectly at line 2354; no Translation copy is identified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. poet. 225
entities.description: Collateral Translation witness not used by Thiemke. DIMEV records two distinct copies, at f. 33v and f. 95; the precise endpoint of each occurrence remains incompletely specified in the repertory data.

subject.description: Pinned repository XML
Encoded title page, two alternate birth openings, shared Life, Translation, and apparatus

object.description: 1919 title page and complete critical reading text

scope.notes: Direct XML-to-scan comparison confirms that the electronic text follows Thiemke's pagination, dual lineation, headings, notes, and Translation boundary.

subject.description: Complete scanned volume
M-based b opening, shared Life, and Translation; excludes the mutually exclusive a opening

scope.notes: Thiemke says M is his basis and that he departs from it only where necessary. M supplies the b opening and cannot supply the separately printed a opening from the alternate redaction family.

subject.description: H readings and parenthesized H lineation in the shared Life and Translation

object.description: Horstmann's Laud Misc. 108 Becket Life and Translation

scope.notes: Thiemke explicitly states that H was available through Horstmann; this collation edge covers the shared continuation and Translation, while a separate transcription edge records Horstmann as the source of Thiemke's continuous a opening. No direct Thiemke-to-Laud-108 inspection edge is asserted.

subject.description: Continuous Laud-family a opening

object.description: Horstmann's Laud Misc. 108 opening used for H

scope.notes: The a opening is absent from M. Direct comparison and Thiemke's method statement identify Horstmann's H transcript as its printed source; this does not assert that Thiemke inspected Laud Misc. 108 directly.

subject.description: Printed Life and Translation of Thomas Becket
Complete scanned volume
W readings and italic W lineation in the b opening, shared Life, and Translation

scope.notes: Thiemke explicitly states that W was available through Black and flags errors in Black's numbering; W does not supply the mutually exclusive a opening, and no direct Thiemke-to-Harley-2277 inspection edge is asserted.

rights.component: Repository XML, markup, and compilation layer
rights.basis: The CME repository contains an Unlicense file whose text speaks of software, while the U-M collection marks underlying texts public domain in the United States. Neither source unambiguously establishes the status of this exact XML markup and compilation layer.

rights.component: U-M CME electronic text as presented by the collection
rights.basis: The official CME collection rights statement says the texts in the collection are public domain in the United States and uses the No Copyright-United States statement. This does not transfer to manuscript photography or unrelated provider layers.

rights.component: 1919 editorial text, introduction, apparatus, and organization
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1919. This publication-date assessment does not by itself license a modern scan or OCR layer outside any separately stated terms.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan images and distributed derivatives
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata supplies the Public Domain Mark 1.0 rights URL.

rights.component: 1887 printed transcript, editorial text, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1887 and Horstmann died in 1912. This excludes the modern scan and metadata layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan images and metadata
rights.basis: The cached item metadata contains no exact license or rights field. The underlying edition is public domain, but no scan-layer license is inferred.

rights.component: 1845 printed transcript, editorial text, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1845. This excludes the modern scan and OCR layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan images, PDF assembly, OCR, and metadata
rights.basis: The cached item metadata contains no exact license or rights field. The underlying edition is public domain, but no scan-layer license is inferred.

rights.component: DIMEV record 6687 and its version 1.0 data representation
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The version 1.0 data release is distributed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: DIMEV record 1507 and its version 1.0 data representation
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The version 1.0 data release is distributed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: DIMEV record 4768 and its version 1.0 data representation
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The version 1.0 data release is distributed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: 2023 dissertation text and deposited PDF
rights.basis: Open repository access does not place a contemporary dissertation in the public domain or supply an open reuse license. Quotation and reuse remain subject to applicable law and repository terms.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of the Life and Translation
rights.basis: The manuscript dates to 1300-1325. This assessment concerns medieval wording only, not photography, catalog encoding, or supplied scans.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of the Life and Translation
rights.basis: The relevant manuscript core is medieval. This excludes modern photography and catalog layers.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of the Life and Translation
rights.basis: The manuscript dates to c. 1300. This excludes photography, catalog text, and ordered reproduction files.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Thirteen manuscript witnesses in two birth-redaction families
editorial_practices.representation: Critical reading text based primarily on M, with documented departures and variants
editorial_practices.description: Thiemke states that Ashmole 43 (M) is the basis of his edition and that he departs from it only where necessary. M supplies the b opening and shared continuation, while the separately printed a opening follows H through Horstmann. Agreement of M with H is decisive where their texts overlap; against M he prefers H, usually with support from S/G/V and the better t-family representatives D/J. No witness supplies both mutually exclusive openings.
editorial_practices.scope: Critical Life and Translation, printed pp. 1-185

editorial_practices.source_feature: Mutually exclusive Laud-family and Harley-family accounts of Gilbert Becket and Thomas's birth
editorial_practices.representation: Both openings printed serially as a and b before the shared continuation
editorial_practices.description: The edition preserves both redactional openings rather than silently choosing one. A coherent Life reads either lines 1a-210a or 1b-156b, then continues with lines 157-2454.

editorial_practices.source_feature: H and W readings unavailable through direct manuscript inspection
editorial_practices.representation: H collated through Horstmann 1887 and W through Black 1845, with source-specific numbering retained
editorial_practices.description: Thiemke identifies M, E, C, S, V, L, D, R, G, J, and B as manuscripts he copied. He instead took H from Horstmann and W from Black, whose numbering errors he notes. The lineage graph therefore stops those two branches at their printed intermediaries before reaching the manuscripts.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Different line-number systems in M, H/Horstmann, and W/Black
editorial_practices.representation: M lineation at left where M is present, H lineation in parentheses, and W lineation in italic type
editorial_practices.description: Thiemke aligns three historical numbering systems rather than normalizing them to one invisible sequence. H supplies the a-opening lineation; M and W begin with the alternative b opening, and none of the three systems covers both openings. CME00142 encodes the resulting milestones and typography, including four inserted lines numbered 2406a-d.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Thiemke's full study and critical edition
editorial_practices.representation: Title page plus complete critical reading text and apparatus; introductory study and back matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text encodes Thiemke's title page and printed pp. 1-185. It retains both alternate openings and the seventy-four-line Translation, but the Translation follows the Life without a fresh HEAD, so structural and repertory evidence must be combined to recover the work boundary.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00142.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; EEBO/IDG; FRONT; BODY/DIV1 and DIV2
evidence.summary: Primary pinned repository evidence for identifiers, Thiemke's title and editorship, Middle English and German editorial layers, alternate openings, critical lineation, Translation boundary, apparatus, and printed pagination.

evidence.citation: Programmatic and direct structural audit of CME00142.xml against Thiemke 1919 and DIMEV records.
evidence.locator: DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, LG, L, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, and P counts; ancestor NOTE filtering; line-number discontinuities; lines 2455-2528
evidence.summary: Confirms 2 DIV1, 3 DIV2, 6 HEAD, 6 LG, 2,748 L, 2,699 NOTE, 186 PB, 464 MILESTONE, and 14 P elements. Four LG are direct running-text verse groups: two in the a opening, one in the b opening, and one shared group that continues across the editorial Life/Translation boundary through line 2528; two further LG containing six L are nested inside the apparatus note at line 2454. The running text has 2,742 lines: a 210-line opening, b 156-line opening, 2,302-line shared Life continuation, and 74-line Translation, including four added lines 2406a-d. Two direct medieval rubric P elements precede and divide the a opening; other prose P elements are editorial matter or nested apparatus.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00142.
evidence.locator: item handle and electronic text
evidence.summary: Persistent public identifier for the U-M electronic text represented by the repository artifact.
notes: Automated retrieval of the item surface returned HTTP 403 during this audit; the stable UMDL handle and local source identifiers remain consistent.

evidence.summary: Identifies the CME collection and states that collection texts are public domain in the United States under No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: complete file as stored in the pinned git object
evidence.summary: Contains Unlicense wording framed in terms of software; its applicability to corpus XML data and editorial markup is not explicit. The file is present in git history but not materialized in this sparse submodule checkout.

evidence.citation: Hermann Thiemke, Die me. Thomas Beket-Legende des Gloucesterlegendars kritisch herausgegeben mit Einleitung, Palaestra 131 (Berlin: Mayer & Müller, 1919).
evidence.locator: introduction pp. I-IX and XLI-LXX; critical text pp. 1-185; especially witness list and editorial method
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for thirteen witnesses, direct and mediated consultation routes, Ashmole 43 as base, redaction families, editorial criteria, lineation, free adaptation of the Quadrilogus, historical authorship and localization arguments, and the exact text encoded by CME00142.

evidence.summary: Complete page-image surrogate used to compare CME pagination, lineation, headings, and the Life/Translation boundary.

evidence.summary: Confirms exact distributed files and supplies Public Domain Mark 1.0 as the item rights URL.

evidence.summary: Printed Harley 2277 transcript edited by William Henry Black that Thiemke used for W, including the Life and Translation.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for item lifemartyrdomoft01unse.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, records 6687, 1507, and 4768, IMEV/NIMEV 4171, 907, and 3064, version 1.0 data release.
evidence.locator: Records.xml records 6687, 1507, and 4768 and linked manuscript records
evidence.summary: Establishes the Laud and Harley Life redactions, discrete seventy-four-line Translation, incipits, subjects, couplet form, modern witness census, and manuscript loci. Its duplicated Laud Misc. 463 Life locus conflicts with a manuscript-specific catalog and is not repeated as fact.

evidence.citation: Tristan B. Taylor, Thomas Becket in the South English Legendaries: Genre, Materiality, and Why the Reader Matters (PhD dissertation, University of Saskatchewan, 2023).
evidence.summary: Supports approximate 1275 composition, anonymous and speculative authorship, the Laud/Harley/Translation distinction and witness counts, Quadrilogus dependence, romance-hagiography-historiography hybridity, and independent incorporation of the 1164 and 1169 legal clauses.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue, MS 223.
evidence.locator: Life ff. 257v-290v; Translation begins 290b; Silvester begins 291b
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, fourteenth-century date, G Life locus, Translation start, and next-item boundary.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA search inventory and all 87 publicly exposed single-image selections matching MS 223.
evidence.locator: 90 search records: 87 mediaType Image and three mediaType Book; Image inventory items 50-51, MS223f291r and MS223f291v
evidence.summary: The 87 single-image selections do not reproduce the whole Becket Life or Translation, but direct inspection identifies f. 291r as Translation text and the opening portion of f. 291v as its end before the Saint Silvester rubric. Together with the catalog's column notation, these images map the Translation from f. 290v (290b) through f. 291r to its ending on f. 291v before Silvester begins (291b). The same search exposes three compound Book objects, documented separately, that do not overlap Becket. This remains date-bounded evidence and is not proof that no additional internal or requestable surrogate exists.

evidence.locator: ff. 16r-20v; IIIF canvases 1-10, provider page indices 0-9
evidence.summary: Official compound Book object and complete published-selection inventory; complete for this selected object, partial relative to MS 223, and with no Becket overlap.

evidence.locator: ff. 33r-42v; IIIF canvases 1-20, provider page indices 0-19

evidence.summary: Official evidence for the composite binding fragments, shelfmark, and fourteenth-century Becket-bearing part. DIMEV identifies the f. 3r-v fragment as only the first sixty Life lines.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Rawl. poet. 225.
evidence.locator: complete catalog TEI; manuscript date 1450-1500; DIMEV records Translation copies at ff. 33v and 95
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, Summary Catalogue number, and second-half-fifteenth-century date. DIMEV separately indexes two Translation occurrences in the same physical manuscript.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts of the West Midlands, Pepys Library MS 2344.
evidence.locator: manuscript record; second half of the fourteenth century; South English Legendary contents
evidence.summary: Scholarly manuscript-specific evidence for the date, contents, and physical manuscript. DIMEV locates the Life at pp. 41-90 and the separated twenty-six-line Translation fragment at p. 528.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Digital Library.
evidence.locator: current digitized-manuscript list, Pepys Librarian contact, image purchase, and publication permission
evidence.summary: MS 2344 is absent from the current public digital library. The page supplies the request contact and states that images are purchased and require permission for publication.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Virtual Reading Room and Imaging Services.
evidence.locator: remote manuscripts enquiry and image-order routes
evidence.summary: Official generic routes for asking Manuscripts staff about MS Add. 3039 and for ordering images. No exact current catalog or public facsimile was established during this audit.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Guidelines for requests for reproductions and licensing only.
evidence.locator: complete request and licensing guide
evidence.summary: Official evidence that image supply and permission or licensing are separate, fee-bearing services; exact rights depend on the requested material and proposed use.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 24078.
evidence.locator: complete catalog record; four fourteenth-century English-legendary binder-waste leaves at ff. 275-278
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, fourteenth-century fragments, and their reuse in a later law volume. DIMEV locates Becket material at ff. 275rb-276va and contains the malformed terminal expression retained in this manifest.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 2810.
evidence.locator: complete record; fourteenth-century manuscript with fifteenth-century additions
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, broad physical date, and blank Digitised Content field. DIMEV supplies the Life ff. 145v-163v and Translation ff. 163v-164 loci.

evidence.citation: College of Arms, Contact Us and Archive and Conservation Departments.
evidence.locator: institutional contact and archive-access routes
evidence.summary: Official generic enquiry evidence for Arundel MS VIII. No exact public catalog, public facsimile, or image-rights statement was established; DIMEV supplies the Life locus ff. 80v-116v and imperfect ending at line 2354.
notes: A second cached official page, source-cache/CME00142/college-of-arms-archive-and-conservation.html, has SHA-256 0d699c0b3f01e9a5ee91570f8441e8925f9111f8698e6fa7f262b4996d1dd466.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian exact shelfmark and identifier searches for MS. Add. C. 38, MS. Eng. poet. c. 3, and MS. Rawl. poet. 225.
evidence.summary: Dated negative public-access evidence: no complete public object was found for the three exact shelfmarks. This is not evidence that Bodleian holds no internal or preservation photography.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, mediated copying order form.
evidence.locator: complete form; email and postal submission, prices, intended-use declaration, and current scan terms
evidence.summary: Official reference-scan request form. It states current prices and applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to scans of out-of-copyright material while requiring further permission for commercial use.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Imaging Services image order form.
evidence.locator: complete form; color TIFF options, prices, and delivery conditions
evidence.summary: Official high-resolution-image order form with per-image prices and current CC BY-NC language for out-of-copyright material, subject to use restrictions.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Imaging Services Terms of Use.
evidence.locator: complete terms; personal, non-commercial, academic, and commercial-use provisions
evidence.summary: Current provider terms permit listed personal, non-commercial, and academic uses while requiring permission for commercial uses. Item-specific delivery correspondence may add conditions.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Image Order Terms and Conditions.
evidence.locator: complete legacy order terms
evidence.summary: A separately linked provider document says Bodleian owns reproductions and customers may not reproduce supplied material without written permission. Because this conflicts with current forms, the manifest preserves it as a separate rights layer requiring clarification.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives shelfmark list, updated 10 October 2025.
evidence.locator: complete 261-page list; Add MS 24078 and Egerton MS 2810 absent
evidence.summary: Dated corroborating evidence that neither collateral shelfmark was in the published digitized-manuscript list; the exact current catalog records also expose no digitized-content links.

open_questions.question: How should DIMEV's Stowe MS 949 Translation locus ff. 141v-144 be reconciled with the current British Library catalog's following-item boundary?
next_steps: Obtain reference images of ff. 141r-144v.
Compare rubrics, incipits, and explicits directly before fixing the final Translation boundary.

open_questions.question: On which side or column of f. 214 does the Life end and the Translation begin in Trinity R.3.25?
next_steps: Transcribe the final Life and opening Translation lines on f. 214r-v against Thiemke lines 2450-2460.
Add column-level locators once verified.

open_questions.question: Can all fifteen active DIMEV 4768 occurrences in fourteen physical manuscripts be reconciled with current manuscript contents and exact surviving loci?
next_steps: Audit each active DIMEV occurrence against manuscript-specific current catalogs, keeping the two Rawlinson poet. 225 copies separate.
Do not count Bodley 779, which appears only as a DIMEV ghost, or Lambeth 223's commented-out unrelated locus as active DIMEV occurrences.
Treat the duplicated Laud Misc. 463 Life locus and Stowe endpoint as source conflicts until physical or image evidence resolves them.
Record damaged, displaced, or non-contiguous Translation copies separately from Life copies.

open_questions.question: Do complete existing digital surrogates exist for Harley 2277, Egerton 1993, Stowe 949, Cotton Cleopatra D IX, or Cotton Julius D IX even though no public copy was found?
next_steps: Ask British Library Digitisation Services whether legacy photography already exists for each codex before commissioning new photography.
Request separate quotations for the full codex and, if necessary, the exact Becket folios.
Use licensing@bl.uk for reproduction information and permissions@bl.uk for the written permission required by the current Imaging Services terms before redistributing supplied images.

open_questions.question: Can Bodleian supply an existing complete digital surrogate of Laud Misc. 463 beyond the one public selected image?
next_steps: Ask mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk whether an internal whole-codex surrogate exists.
If none exists, request separate whole-codex and ff. 141r-157rb quotations.
Obtain written publication and redistribution terms for any supplied files.

open_questions.question: Can Lambeth Palace Library supply an existing complete digital surrogate of MS 223 beyond its 87 public single-image records and three compound selections?
next_steps: Ask Imaging Services whether legacy whole-codex photography exists.
If none exists, request separate whole-codex and ff. 257v-291v quotations.
Obtain written publication and redistribution permission before sharing supplied images.

open_questions.question: How should conflicting historical and current dates for Laud Misc. 108, CCCC MS 145, Stowe MS 949, Cotton Cleopatra D IX, Trinity R.3.25, and Lambeth MS 223 be reconciled?
next_steps: Retain each date as an attributed statement rather than collapsing disagreements into a synthetic range.
Consult current codicological literature and, where possible, dated-document and scribal evidence for the relevant production units.
Keep codex-wide dates distinct from dates assigned to the Becket-bearing booklet or scribal layer.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense dedication expressly cover corpus XML data and editorial markup, or only software?
next_steps: Seek a repository-level data-specific rights clarification.
Keep the XML/markup layer separate from U-M's NoC-US statement for collection texts and from the public-domain source edition.
